questions.question: Why can't a transformer generate raw audio waveforms directly?
options: A 5-second clip at 16-48 kHz is 80-240k samples — no transformer attends to that sequence; instead a neural codec (Encodec, SoundStream, DAC) compresses audio to discrete tokens at 50-75 Hz and the generator models tokens
questions.explanation: This codec-plus-generator substrate underlies every production audio model in 2026 — TTS, music, and sound design all run on it.

questions.question: How does a neural codec turn waveforms into tokens?
options: A convolutional encoder compresses the waveform to per-timestep vectors; residual vector quantization converts each into a cascade of K codebook indices (e.g., 8 RVQ codebooks at 75 Hz = 600 tokens/sec at 2 kbps for 24 kHz audio); a decoder reverses it
Each sample is rounded to 8 bits
questions.explanation: RVQ's residual cascade is what achieves usable quality at low bitrate — each successive codebook refines the previous level's error.

questions.question: What are the two generative paradigms on top of codec tokens?
options: Token-autoregressive (decoder-only transformer over flattened RVQ streams — MusicGen's delayed-parallel pattern, VALL-E's text + 3s voice sample) and latent diffusion / flow matching over continuous audio latents (Stable Audio 2.5, AudioLDM 2)
questions.explanation: MusicGen's "delayed parallel" trick emits the K codebook streams simultaneously with per-stream offsets, sidestepping the cost of fully flattening 600 tokens/sec.

questions.question: Why does token-AR dominate speech while flow matching wins for music?
options: Token-AR is naturally causal and streams well — essential for conversational TTS latency (~200-300ms first token); flow matching gives faster batch inference and cleaner samples for music, where streaming matters less
questions.explanation: The deployment shape picks the paradigm: ElevenLabs and GPT-4o audio stream token-by-token; Stable Audio 2.5 renders a minute of music in ~10s offline.

questions.question: What do the three audio-generation task families share?
options: TTS (VALL-E, NaturalSpeech 3), music (MusicGen, Suno, Stable Audio), and sound design / Foley (AudioGen, AudioLDM 2) all run on the same substrate: neural codec + token-AR or diffusion generator
They share only the sample rate
questions.explanation: The differences are distributional: clean speech is narrow-band with strong phonetic structure (easy for token-AR); music's far broader distribution rewards diffusion's mode coverage.
